publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, diaries, minutes, notes, memoranda, reports, and other papers.
did.abstract: Correspondence and other papers relating to Ries' career as professor of geology at Cornell University.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Heinrich Ries was professor of geology at Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Mainly personal and professional correspondence, especially concerning Cornell Geology Department administration and conflicts; correspondents include Henry Platt Cushing, Edmund Ezra Day, Livingston Farrand, Adam C. Gill, Gilbert D. Harris, Charles W. Merriam, Charles S. Prosser, Floyd K. Richtmyer, Ralph S. Tarr, Oscar D. von Engeln, Henry Shaler Williams, and others. Also, one diary kept by Ries (ca. 1914-1939) and one by Henry S. Williams (1889-1892, 1904-1908) concerning departmental affairs, typescript departmental minutes (1904-1914), laboratory notes (1904-1909), memoranda, enrollment statistics, reports, notes, condolence letters, and other items relating to Ries, Williams, and the Geology Department at Cornell University.

relatedmaterial.head: NOTES
relatedmaterial.p: Related collection: Mary Eleanor Davis. Student Notebooks, #37-5-1244.
Related collection: Donald T. Ries. Papers, #1350.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Heinrich Ries papers, #14-15-691. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Letters of condolence from former graduate students and other associates
Geology Department (notebook): H.S. Williams, Department Matters
Geology Department minutes; Geological Conferences minutes
Minutes of meeting at which U.D. proposed an all-year course
